claims.claim: Illmari Vaino is the founder of Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Gin-Soaked Rag is associated with the Sin and Tonic.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Dura Oil Drinker is a mechanic associated with Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Elias Ramsey serves as the quartermaster of Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: The Roundhat Gang gang is run by Tywelwyn Leatherhide.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Tywelwyn Leatherhide ordered materials from Embercall Industries, which is associated with the Pyrelight faction.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The faction known as Pyrelight operates a front company called Embercall Industries.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The law enforcement body operating in the area is called the Threshold Authority, also known as the TA.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Threshold Authority is suspected of being bought out by either the Roundhat Gang gang or Embercall Industries slash Pyrelight.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Augers are creatures created through a divination ritual and are an offshoot of that process; they can be fed to enhance divination.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Iconoclasm possesses a company car available for use on missions.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Ginny's physical appearance consists of a suit vest and dress pants, but instead of a head, has a twisted-up dish rag that can twist to face different directions and appears to squeeze out liquid as it moves.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Ginny works the bar and cooking station at the establishment, serving as staff in the absence of Illmari.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Illmari is normally responsible for cooking food at the establishment and is more skilled at preparing human food than Ginny.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The twins are not running a magic item shop, but are two people who happen to be particularly good at crafting.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The radar dish previously caused things to come alive and attack when activated.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The NPC crafter's workshop is the room where rocket hammers are made.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The radar dish is believed to be a device for receiving a signal.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anouk Marchal keeps a Rolodex-like collection of index cards containing contacts and job leads, which she uses to refer people to work opportunities.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: There is a scrapyard located one district over from the party's current location in Sableclutch, run by an old friend of Anouk Marchal's who regularly hires couriers and other workers.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Arbiters are empowered by the The Scale to conduct bounty hunting, and the The Scale does not hire outside individuals for such work.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The city of Helya has three police organizations that post bounties for outstanding warrants.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In Hallia, particularly strong magic set up by the host prevents people from teleporting or sliding freely between city districts; residents must use public transit to travel between areas.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Hallia has a trolley system that travels along a route called the Horizon, connecting different numbered districts such as Star 12.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The neighborhood where the Sin and Tonic is located is a commercialized, generally poor but not seedy area of Helya.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The starward districts of Helya feature older, poorly maintained buildings that were constructed when the tram line was first built, in anticipation of a boom that never materialized.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Undertable is a pawn shop located in the Star 12 district of Helya, tucked away enough to avoid drawing attention but accessible enough to reach.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anzu owns a normal-sized raven named Othello.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Undertable is a shop squeezed into a narrow gap between two buildings, too large to be an alleyway but too small for a standard building, resulting in a weirdly narrow, squished structure.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Undertable has no windows facing the street like a regular shop front, only a door with a handwritten sign reading 'under table' accompanied by a crude drawing of a table.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The interior of the Undertable is a cramped room with tables along the walls and a large central table, all stacked with a wide variety of goods including kitchen appliances, loose jewelry, radios in various states of deconstruction, and a large number of firearms.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Undertable stocks enough firearms that a license would normally be required, but it is implied the proprietor does not hold one.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler is a centaur and the proprietor of the Undertable, as well as a fence known to Johnny.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler stands approximately nine feet tall at full centaur height, tall enough that he must stoop beneath the ceiling of the Undertable.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler's appearance is characterized by extensive jewelry: multiple yellowish metal bands on both arms, gems braided into his tail, flare gauges in his ears with gems embedded in them, and a long beard braided in the same style as his tail with gems throughout.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler extends a friends-and-associates discount to Johnny's companions in Iconoclasm as a personal favor to Johnny.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler is acquainted with individuals named Donnie and Red, and is willing to pass messages between them.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Argyle's eyes, normally gold in color, can shift to a bright white or bluish color under certain conditions.
claims.speaker: Argyle

claims.claim: Belvedere is a place where children receive an education, and it functions as a school amongst other things.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Children in Belvedere do not ordinarily receive permission to travel out into the field hundreds of miles away from Belvedere; such cases are considered special exceptions.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch is a neighborhood located near the party's base of operations, in the city of Hallia.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a scrapyard located in the Sableclutch neighborhood.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Hallia has a public tram transportation system.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch is not a prosperous part of town.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch is notable for having numerous religious areas, including many churches.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The churches in Sableclutch are mostly dedicated to Iridescent Host, with a smattering of other gods also represented.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The scrapyard is located on the voidward strata of the city of Hallia, accessible by tram.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The voidward direction of Hallia passes through slums before reaching undeveloped land that feels like the far outskirts, despite being geographically close to the city center.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The voidward part of Hallia is home primarily to factories that dump waste into the river running through the city, situated there to avoid legal liability and harm to residents.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A river runs through the city of Hallia.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a scrapyard located in the voidward industrial district of Hallia, characterized by mountains of trash and large metal-crushing machinery.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A contact named Copperjaw operates at or is associated with Sableclutch Scrap, and is known to the character Anouk.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is a dwarven man who operates a Crusher.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw has a mashed-up, misshapen nose and a black eye, indicating he regularly gets into fights.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw appears to be at the end of middle age or beginning of elderly, which by dwarven reckoning is approximately 120–130 years old.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In dwarven society, the end of middle age corresponds to roughly 120–130 years of age.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw's bottom jaw has been replaced with a crude copper prosthetic, roughly shaped like a jaw, which is the origin of his name.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A person named Anaïs directed the party to seek out Copperjaw for work, indicating Copperjaw regularly hires people for jobs.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is the kind of person who does not make allowances for age when testing someone's strength in a handshake.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The employer operates out of a converted storage container used as an office.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The handcuffs used to secure the suitcase are magical in nature.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The locked metal suitcase is extraordinarily heavy — heavy enough that dropping it would make a small crater in the ground.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw operates a scrapyard.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw has a Giant Crusher available at his scrapyard, which he uses to deter unwanted visitors.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Automobiles exist in this setting, and as a consequence tire companies also exist.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A publication called the Korath Guide exists in this setting, analogous to a restaurant/travel guide.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Diamond District is a relatively upscale part of the city, known for jewelers and fine metal shops, and is located near high-end residential areas.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The setting does not have television; radio shows exist instead.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Tarv is a cross-sphere entity or concept, meaning it exists across multiple spheres/worlds.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Tarv's presence in this sphere is explained as convergent evolution — the setting independently developed the same concept rather than through direct cross-sphere transmission.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The setting is described as a magical society where bird people and other non-human races are a common sight and do not attract unusual attention.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The party's route back passes through a trolley area, suggesting the city has a trolley transit system.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A goblin pickpocket was encountered in an alley; the goblin has a greenish skin tone, appears to be in their late 20s, and is shorter than a human adult.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Threshold Authority officers wear a greenish uniform with a large 'TA' insignia on it.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Black Line is a law enforcement organization that generally polices the nicer parts of the city.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Black Line officers stand out as unusual when present in less affluent parts of the city, as their jurisdiction covers nicer districts.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Black Line officers wear full uniforms and carry batons as their standard sidearm, rather than firearms.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline is an organization or service that offers paid subscriptions, and law enforcement officers are associated with it — implying policing services are provided on a subscription basis in this setting.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a junkyard or scrap business located in the area where the officers regularly patrol.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline agents carry identification badges bearing an identification number.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline badges are not commonly encountered by local law enforcement, suggesting the company does not frequently operate in the same areas.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline is a private law enforcement company that operates in at least some districts of the city.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is a named individual known to Blackline agents, who report to or communicate with them.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw has been known to hire people, including children, to deliver important items or cases.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Officer Smith is a named officer of the local law enforcement.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Officer Smith's partner, known as Mute, is unable to speak due to a bad accident sustained in a scuffle with a local gang.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A trolley station is located in or near the industrial district with factories and scrapyards.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area the party is traveling through has winding streets with no traffic cops, because traffic enforcement would cost money and there is no clear party to fund it.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area near the trolley station is characterized by trucks carting goods in and out of factories and scrapyards.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The station has a tram (trolley) system that operates on a fixed route, with trams arriving periodically at designated stops.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Goblinoid creatures are commonly employed as workers in the factories of the area, and are a visible presence among the working population.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Skinwalkers are difficult to identify by sight alone, requiring a significantly higher DC check than simply determining whether someone is mute.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Masque District is an area of the city that contains hotels and serves as a destination for tourists.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram system has named stations, including at least 'Void 2' and 'Zero' (also called 'Prime'), suggesting a numbered or tiered station naming convention.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram line is operated by Prime Meridian.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram stop the party disembarks at is the central location on its line, making it a high-traffic stop.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The residential district the party is heading to is wealthy enough that all residents can afford private cars, meaning public transport does not service that part of the city.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A taxi fare into the wealthy residential district costs approximately three silver pieces.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The two figures, referred to by the party as Smith and Mute, were shapeshifters capable of growing approximately a foot and a half in size and shedding their disguised appearance.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In their true form, Smith and Mute appear as large, pink, fleshy, naked mole rat-like creatures whose clothes melt into their bodies during transformation.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In their true form, Smith and Mute have faces where the eyes sink into the head leaving only empty sockets, and most other facial features melt back into the skin.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster
